query: I'm starting a multiplayer game and need to decide whether the server owns all the gameplay objects or if peers can each own their own — and how to keep that flexible if I switch to peer-to-peer later
rationale: direct: topology + per-object authority, topology-as-config -> topology-and-authority.md

query: Each participant runs its own copy of the world bound to an address and they talk to each other. How should I structure who accepts connections and who simulates what?
rationale: implicit: node-graph model, node behavior (accept/simulate/replicate) -> topology-and-authority.md

query: When a player's position changes on the host, how do I get only the changed objects out to the other machines instead of broadcasting the whole world every frame?
rationale: direct: per-frame deltas, relevancy, owner-watches-changes -> state-replication.md / delta-and-snapshots.md

query: How do I mark which entities and which of their components actually get sent over the wire? Right now I think I'm syncing everything by accident.
rationale: direct: two-level opt-in (component capability + per-object flag) -> state-replication.md

query: My replicated structs send the whole blob even when one float changed. How do I get it to only transmit the members that actually changed?
rationale: direct: whole-blob default vs per-field layout description -> state-replication.md

query: A new client just connected mid-match and its world is garbage — I'm only sending the per-frame changes. What am I missing?
rationale: direct: delta needs a baseline; new connection needs a full snapshot first -> delta-and-snapshots.md

query: Can I reuse my save-game serialization to send and apply state updates over the connection, or do I need a separate path?
rationale: implicit: replication reuses the save/load dump+apply path -> delta-and-snapshots.md

query: I built everything on UDP and now some critical messages get dropped while others I don't care about clog the line. How should I decide reliability per message?
rationale: direct: per-packet-type delivery guarantee, head-of-line tradeoff -> transport-and-channels.md

query: My connection setup opens a second channel every time the response gets lost and the client retries. How should the handshake behave?
rationale: direct: idempotent request/accept handshake, pipe ids -> transport-and-channels.md

query: I want to run a server and a client in the same process to debug, then deploy them to separate machines without changing code. How do I make local talk behave like remote?
rationale: direct: virtual networking / in-process short-circuit -> dev-iteration-and-observability.md

query: How can I fake 150ms of lag and a bandwidth cap in the editor so I can reproduce a desync bug without a real bad network?
rationale: direct: injected latency/bandwidth + packet inspection -> dev-iteration-and-observability.md

query: I need to fire a remote event at a specific entity on the other side and sometimes the order of these events matters and sometimes it doesn't. How should I send them?
rationale: implicit: replicate event by id, ordered vs unordered delivery -> state-replication.md / transport-and-channels.md

query: Both my host and a client think they own the same crate and they keep snapping it back and forth. How do I resolve this?
rationale: direct: singular authority per object -> topology-and-authority.md

query: I want one project that ships as single-player but can also run multiplayer by having one process play all the roles. How do I branch on that?
rationale: implicit: topology-switch catch-all path runs all branches -> dev-iteration-and-observability.md

query: Restructure this CPU particle array from array-of-structs to struct-of-arrays so the update loop stops missing cache
rationale: near-miss: in-engine memory layout -> data-oriented-design-guide, not networking

query: Should an entity be able to hold two of the same component, like two colliders, or model it differently in my ECS?
rationale: near-miss: component model design -> ecs-guide, not networking

query: How do I give every asset and object a stable GUID so references survive save/load and renaming?
rationale: near-miss: typed object/id model -> data-model-guide, not networking (networking only reuses those ids)

query: I need to insert the right resource barriers between my render passes and alias transient targets on the GPU
rationale: near-miss: GPU frame graph -> gpu-rendering-guide, not networking
